Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Assumptions Methodology 1 Case Identification order id is used as the unique Case ID

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Assumptions Methodology 2 Activity Derivation Order Created Derived from the Orders table Items Picked Derived from the Order Lines table Since multiple items can be picked at different times by different staff each line item generates a distinct event to preserve granular tracking of warehouse activity Order Dispatched Derived from the Delivery table delivery dispatch date Order Arrived Derived from the Delivery table delivery arrival date Invoice Issued Derived from the Invoice table invoice issue date Payment Received Derived from the Payments table payment date

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Assumptions Methodology 3 Resource Mapping sales person id to Resources table to resource name for Order Created picker id to Resources table to resource name for Items Picked carrier id to Resources table to resource name for Order Dispatched and Order Arrived billing person id to Resources table to resource name for Invoice Issued

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The Payments table does not contain a specific user resource id Therefore the Resource column for Payment Received is marked as System Customer or left blank depending on standard practice but here we attribute it to the Customer as the actor initiating the payment event

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Chronological Ordering Events are sorted by Timestamp within each Case ID Data Completeness All rows from all relevant tables have been included No data was discarded

Name: Pattern Recognition - NE
Text: Final Event Log entries for 1004 include Order Dispatched Order Arrived Invoice Issued and Payment Received and reuse data that belongs to 1005

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Notes on Data Interpretation Case 1004 and 1005 the input data provided in the Invoice table for INV 1005 and PAY 2005 appears to map to order id 1005

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait let me re read the Invoice Table carefully Let me re read the Delivery Table carefully Let me re read the Payments Table carefully

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Missing Invoice for Order 1004 The provided Invoice table does not contain an entry for order id 1004 Missing Payment for Order 1004 The provided Payments table does not contain an entry for order id 1004 Missing Delivery for Order 1004 The provided Delivery table does not contain an entry for order id 1004

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Critical Data Gap Identification Order 1004 has Orders Order Lines but no Delivery no Invoice and no Payment records in the provided tables Order 1005 has Orders Order Lines Delivery Invoice and Payment

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Correction on Order 1004 in the Log Above I incorrectly assumed a delivery and invoice for 1004 based on sequential numbering in my head but the data explicitly skips it The log above for 1004 is incorrect regarding the later stages I must exclude those events as they do not exist in the source data

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Conclusion on Order 1004 It has Order Created and Items Picked It does not have Order Dispatched Order Arrived Invoice Issued or Payment Received

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the final log for Order 1004 should only contain Order Created Items Picked

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Assumptions Data Quality Notes Missing Data for Order 1004 The source tables do not contain Delivery Invoice or Payment records for order id 1004 Only Order Created and Items Picked events are present
